claims.claim: Deepgram exposes /v1/listen and /v2/listen for streaming vs the async/batch path
claims.found: line 1984 '/v1/listen' = 'Low-latency streaming STT over WebSocket' (non-Flux); line 1993 '/v2/listen' = Flux conversational streaming. BOTH endpoints are streaming WebSocket; the ebook describes NO async/batch listen path at these lines. The note's 'vs the async/batch path' framing misrepresents what these two endpoints are.
claims.correction: Both /v1/listen and /v2/listen are streaming endpoints (v1=regular streaming non-Flux, v2=Flux conversational streaming). Remove the 'vs the async/batch path' contrast -- the ebook does not document an async/batch listen endpoint here.

claims.claim: Modal chose batch-style segment-then-transcribe (Parakeet) over streaming and still hit ~1s v2v
claims.found: line 33 supports the Parakeet segment-then-transcribe-faster-than-streaming claim and 'only thing that matters... is the final transcript time'. But the '~1s v2v' figure is at line 26 ('Apps target ~1 second or less'), not line 33.
claims.correction: modal-v2v:26 for the ~1s v2v target; modal-v2v:33 for the segment-then-transcribe choice.

claims.claim: Whisper/OpenAI are inherently batch and are wrapped (Pipecat SegmentedSTTService, LiveKit StreamAdapter)
claims.found: StreamAdapter confirmed in stream_adapter.py: wraps a batch STT (calls recognize() per VAD segment); StreamAdapterWrapper docstring 'recognize speech from vad'. Consistent with claim.

summary: 5 code_refs all SUPPORTED. deepgram glossary and together-talk figures SUPPORTED. One WRONG: the note frames /v1/listen and /v2/listen as 'streaming vs the async/batch path', but per deepgram-ebook:1981-1993 both are streaming WebSocket endpoints (v1 regular streaming, v2 Flux conversational) -- no async/batch listen endpoint is documented there. One MISCITED: the ~1s v2v figure is at modal-v2v:26 not :33 (:33 supports the segment-then-transcribe claim only).
